content.claim: Hungary exhibits among the highest OECD disparities in mathematics performance between top and bottom socioeconomic quartiles, and among top readers only 1% come from deprived backgrounds versus 3% OECD average, indicating selective tracks concentrate high performance among already-advantaged students.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: PISA does not directly attribute inequality to 6/8-year gimnázium tracks versus other institutional features
Cross-country comparison reflects many structural differences beyond tracking age
Snapshot years may not reveal trends
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
